title: Quelle heure est-il ?
description: Tell time in both everyday and official 24-hour format — essential for the train/flight announcements that TCF listening sections love to test.

content.subtitle: Module 3 · Leçon 2
content.body: Telling time well is essential for schedules, appointments, and — critically — the short audio announcements about train and flight times that show up constantly in TCF listening sections.

content.explanation: French always uses "Il est" (it is) to state the time, followed by the number and "heure(s)". Note "heure" stays singular only for one o'clock.

content.word: et quart / et demie / moins le quart
content.nativeTranslation: et quart / et demie / moins le quart
content.englishTranslation: quarter past / half past / quarter to
content.pronunciation: ay kar / ay duh-MEE / mwan luh kar
content.ipa: e kaʁ / e dəmi / mwɛ̃ lə kaʁ
content.exampleSentence: Il est quatre heures et quart.
content.nativeExample: It is a quarter past four.

content.word: du matin / de l'après-midi / du soir
content.nativeTranslation: du matin / de l'après-midi / du soir
content.englishTranslation: in the morning / in the afternoon / in the evening
content.pronunciation: dew mah-TAN / duh lah-preh-mee-DEE / dew swahr
content.ipa: dy matɛ̃ / də lapʁɛmidi / dy swaʁ
content.exampleSentence: Le magasin ouvre à neuf heures du matin.
content.nativeExample: The store opens at nine in the morning.

content.explanation: Timetables, official announcements, and TEF/TCF audio use the 24-hour clock, with minutes stated directly (no "et quart"/"moins le quart").

content.body: "Le train pour Lyon part à quinze heures trente, quai numéro deux." — this exact sentence pattern (transport + official time + platform/gate) is the single most common TCF listening announcement format. Learn to catch the time and the location.

content.prompt: Comment dit-on "9:15 AM" en heure officielle ?

content.hint: The fixed phrase used to state time

content.prompt: À quelle heure part le train ?
Match each time expression to its English meaning.

content.statement: En heure officielle, on dit "et quart" et "moins le quart".

content.prompt: Arrange the words to say "It is a quarter to five."
Select every expression related to telling time.

lines.speaker: Voyageur
lines.text: Excusez-moi, à quelle heure part le train pour Lyon ?
lines.translation: Excuse me, what time does the train to Lyon leave?

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Il part à quinze heures trente, quai numéro deux.
lines.translation: It leaves at 3:30 PM, platform two.

content.prompt: Repeat this train announcement out loud.
content.targetText: Le train pour Lyon part à quinze heures trente, quai numéro deux.

content.prompt: Le magasin ferme à quelle heure ?

content.intro: Two quick questions before the summary.

content.heading: Key takeaways
points: "Il est..." is always used to state the time
Everyday time: et quart, et demie, moins le quart
Official/24h time: minutes stated directly (quinze heures trente = 15h30)
Transport announcements follow a fixed pattern: mode + destination + official time + platform/gate

content.title: Leçon terminée !
content.message: Bravo ! You can now tell time in both everyday and official French — and understand transit announcements.
